key_classes.name: register_connector
key_classes.responsibility: Module init hook: pops vLLM's built-in 'MultiConnector' from KVConnectorFactory._registry and re-registers it pointing at AscendMultiConnector, then registers MooncakeConnectorV1/MooncakeHybridConnector/MooncakeLayerwiseConnector/AscendStoreConnector etc. by name. This is *how* config strings get routed to ascend classes.

key_classes.name: AscendMultiConnector
key_classes.responsibility: Subclass of vLLM MultiConnector + SupportsHMA. Inherits the 'load from first matching sub-connector, save to all' fan-out. Adds: (1) HMA assertion in __init__; (2) update_state_after_alloc override so a MooncakeLayerwiseConnector ALWAYS receives the real blocks (layerwise push needs them even when not the 'chosen' loader); (3) request_finished_all_groups HMA variant.

key_classes.name: MultiConnector
key_classes.responsibility: Base (vLLM) fan-out wrapper around a list self._connectors. Scheduler-side get_num_new_matched_tokens polls each child in config order; first child returning toks>0 is recorded in _requests_to_connector and owns the load. Saves go to all children. Wraps per-child metadata in MultiKVConnectorMetadata.

key_classes.name: MooncakeLayerwiseConnector
key_classes.responsibility: A KVConnectorBase_V1 + SupportsHMA. Thin facade: by KVConnectorRole it owns either a MooncakeLayerwiseConnectorScheduler (scheduler proc) or a MooncakeLayerwiseConnectorWorker (worker proc) and forwards every base-class hook to it. This is the canonical vLLM connector scheduler/worker split.

key_classes.name: MooncakeLayerwiseConnectorScheduler
key_classes.responsibility: Scheduler-side. Reads request.kv_transfer_params: do_remote_prefill (this node is the *decoder*, must pull whole prompt KV from a remote prefiller) vs do_remote_decode (this node is the *prefiller*, must push KV to a remote decoder). Maintains _reqs_need_recv / _reqs_need_send_layerwise, POSTs a handshake (remote_host/port/engine_id/block_ids) to the proxy metaserver, and emits ReqMeta into the per-step connector metadata.

key_classes.name: MooncakeLayerwiseConnectorWorker
key_classes.responsibility: Worker-side. Registers KV cache buffers with the mooncake engine; runs background send/recv threads. start_load_kv (consumer registers recving reqs / producer resolves block mappings) and save_kv_layer (producer enqueues each layer's KV the moment it's computed = layer-by-layer push) are the two hooks the model-runner forward loop calls. get_finished reports completed transfers back to the scheduler so blocks can be freed.

key_classes.name: MooncakeLayerwiseConnectorMetadata / ReqMeta
key_classes.responsibility: The scheduler->worker message. MooncakeLayerwiseConnectorMetadata.requests maps req_id->ReqMeta; ReqMeta carries local_block_ids and the remote side's engine_id/host/port/block_ids/layer_metadata pulled from kv_transfer_params. This is the 'metadata' channel of the connector contract.

key_classes.name: GlobalTE
key_classes.responsibility: Process-wide singleton holding one mooncake TransferEngine, lazily initialized under a double-checked lock with backend 'ascend' and 'P2PHANDSHAKE' mode. register_buffer() registers KV tensor memory regions once for RDMA-style P2P transfer.

key_classes.name: KVCacheSendingLayerThread
key_classes.responsibility: Background daemon. Consumes SendTask items off a queue; get_transfer_meta turns (local block_ids, remote block_ids, base addresses, block_len) into flat src/dst/length lists, then _transfer_kv_cache invokes the mooncake engine to do the cross-node P2P copy. group_concurrent_contiguous batches adjacent blocks into single transfers.

key_classes.name: SharedProxyScheduler
key_classes.responsibility: Centralized PD-aware load balancer shared by all uvicorn workers. Two RolePools (PREFILL, DECODE), each a least-loaded lazy-deletion min-heap. _priority: prefill = active_tokens + 0.3*active_kv_cache, decode = active_tokens. begin_request/reserve_prefill_kv/pick_decoder/release_* mutate per-server load and re-heap.

key_classes.name: assign_instances / handle_completions_impl
key_classes.responsibility: Request orchestration. assign_instances picks a prefiller, sends a prefill-only request (max_tokens=1, do_remote_decode=True), reads back kv_transfer_params (remote_block_ids/engine_id/host/port), picks a decoder, and streams generation from the decoder. handle_completions_impl wires streaming + 'recomputed' retry (reassign on KV eviction).

key_classes.name: KVPoolScheduler
key_classes.responsibility: ★ Scheduler-side connector for the KV-pool/ascend_store path. ch10 uses ONLY its cache-hit query: get_num_new_matched_tokens calls client.lookup to learn how many of THIS request's prompt tokens already live in the external KV pool, then asks vLLM to allocate exactly the gap (need_to_allocate) and load from the pool — affinity routing. Its store/pool tick machinery is deferred to ch11.

key_classes.name: LookupKeyClient
key_classes.responsibility: zmq REQ client to the pool's lookup RPC socket. lookup(token_len, block_hashes, kv_cache_group_ids) msgpack-encodes the request's per-block hashes and returns an int = number of externally-cached tokens (the cache-hit prefix length).

elide: UCMConnector / LMCacheAscendConnector / SimpleCPUOffloadConnector registrations (L57-L81) omitted — same register_connector(name, module, cls) pattern, not on the PD / affinity path.
embed_excerpts.code:
def register_connector():
    # override multi_connector as ascend_multi_connector
    if "MultiConnector" in KVConnectorFactory._registry:
        KVConnectorFactory._registry.pop("MultiConnector")
    KVConnectorFactory.register_connector(
        "MultiConnector", "vllm_ascend.distributed.kv_transfer.ascend_multi_connector", "AscendMultiConnector"
    )

    KVConnectorFactory.register_connector(
        "MooncakeConnectorV1", "vllm_ascend.distributed.kv_transfer.kv_p2p.mooncake_connector", "MooncakeConnector"
    )

    KVConnectorFactory.register_connector(
        "MooncakeHybridConnector",
        "vllm_ascend.distributed.kv_transfer.kv_p2p.mooncake_hybrid_connector",
        "MooncakeConnector",
    )

    KVConnectorFactory.register_connector(
        "MooncakeConnectorStoreV1",
        "vllm_ascend.distributed.kv_transfer.kv_pool.ascend_store.ascend_store_connector",
        "AscendStoreConnector",
    )

    KVConnectorFactory.register_connector(
        "AscendStoreConnector",
        "vllm_ascend.distributed.kv_transfer.kv_pool.ascend_store.ascend_store_connector",
        "AscendStoreConnector",
    )

    KVConnectorFactory.register_connector(
        "MooncakeLayerwiseConnector",
        "vllm_ascend.distributed.kv_transfer.kv_p2p.mooncake_layerwise_connector",
        "MooncakeLayerwiseConnector",
    )

elide: request_finished_all_groups (L43-L67) — HMA aggregate of per-connector async-save counts; shown separately in design_decisions.

embed_excerpts.code:
def get_num_new_matched_tokens(self, request: "Request", num_computed_tokens: int) -> tuple[int, bool]:
        """
        For remote prefill, pull all prompt blocks from remote
        asynchronously relative to engine execution.
        """
        params = request.kv_transfer_params

        if params is not None and params.get("do_remote_prefill"):
            # Remote prefill: get all prompt blocks from remote.
            assert num_computed_tokens % min(self.block_size) == 0
            # Note: We use the full token count as transmit data here.
            count = max(len(request.prompt_token_ids) - num_computed_tokens, 0)
            return count, count > 0

        # No remote prefill for this request.
        return 0, False

elide: TLS/exception-callback plumbing trimmed; the kv_transfer_params handshake dict and the recv/send branch split are the load-bearing parts.
embed_excerpts.code:
def update_state_after_alloc(self, request: "Request", blocks: "KVCacheBlocks", num_external_tokens: int):
        params = request.kv_transfer_params

        if params is not None and params.get("do_remote_prefill"):
            do_virtual = params.get("do_virtual", False)
            local_block_ids = (blocks.get_block_ids()) if num_external_tokens > 0 else []
            remote_cached_tokens = request.num_computed_tokens
            self._reqs_need_recv[request.request_id] = (
                request,
                [],  # request._all_token_ids,
                local_block_ids,
            )
            params["do_remote_prefill"] = False
            external_req_id = get_external_request_id(request.request_id)
            kv_transfer_params = dict(
                token_ids=[],
                request_id=external_req_id,
                do_remote_prefill=False,
                do_remote_decode=True,
                remote_block_ids=local_block_ids,
                remote_block_size=self.block_size,
                remote_engine_id=self.engine_id,
                remote_host=self.side_channel_host,
                remote_port=self.side_channel_port,
                remote_tp_size=self.vllm_config.parallel_config.tensor_parallel_size,
                remote_pcp_size=self.vllm_config.parallel_config.prefill_context_parallel_size,
                remote_dcp_size=self.vllm_config.parallel_config.decode_context_parallel_size,
                remote_cached_tokens=remote_cached_tokens,
            )
            if not do_virtual:
                future = self.executor.submit(
                    self._access_metaserver, url=params.get("metaserver", None), message=kv_transfer_params
                )
                # ... done-callback elided ...

        # Layerwise prefiller add request need send
        if params is not None and params.get("do_remote_decode"):
            local_block_ids = list(blocks.get_block_ids())
            remote_cache_tokens = params["remote_cached_tokens"]
            self._reqs_need_send_layerwise[request.request_id] = SendReqInfo(
                local_block_ids=local_block_ids,
                local_transferred_tokens=remote_cache_tokens,
                local_computed_tokens=0,
                request=request,
            )

embed_excerpts.code:
def save_kv_layer(
        self,
        layer_name: str,
        kv_layer: list[torch.Tensor],
        attn_metadata: "AttentionMetadata",
        connector_metadata: MooncakeLayerwiseConnectorMetadata,
        **kwargs,
    ) -> None:
        """MooncakeLayerwiseConnector does not save explicitly."""
        if self.vllm_config.kv_transfer_config.is_kv_producer and connector_metadata.requests.keys():
            if self.current_layer >= self.total_layers:
                self.current_layer += 1
                return
            # ... record reshape_cache_event; optional resharding/quant elided ...
            send_task = connector_metadata.send_task
            layer_group_idx = self.layer_metadata[layer_name].tensor_group_idx[0]
            # (this layer is pushed the instant it is computed = layer-by-layer pipelining)
            layer_send_task = SendTask(
                wait_event=reshape_cache_event,
                k_cache=keys,
                v_cache=values,
                layer_idx=self.current_layer,
                layer_name=layer_name,
                group_rearrange_block_ids=send_task.group_rearrange_block_ids,
            )
            for req_id, req_meta in connector_metadata.requests.items():
                if len(req_meta.local_block_ids[layer_group_idx]) == 0:
                    continue
                req_meta_update = self.update_decoder_info(req_id, req_meta)
                layer_send_task.send_request[req_id] = req_meta_update

            self.kv_send_layer_thread.send_queue.put(layer_send_task)
            self.current_layer += 1

embed_excerpts.code:
def group_concurrent_contiguous(
    src: list[int], dst: list[int] | None = None
) -> tuple[list[npt.NDArray[np.int64]], list[npt.NDArray[np.int64]]]:
    """Vectorised NumPy implementation."""
    # ... src-only branch elided ...
    src_indices = np.array(src, dtype=np.int64)
    dst_indices = np.array(dst, dtype=np.int64)
    if src_indices.size == 0:
        return [], []
    # break wherever EITHER local or remote block ids stop being consecutive
    brk = np.where((np.diff(src_indices) != 1) | (np.diff(dst_indices) != 1))[0] + 1
    src_groups = np.split(src_indices, brk)
    dst_groups = np.split(dst_indices, brk)
    return [g.tolist() for g in src_groups], [g.tolist() for g in dst_groups]

def build_prefill_request(req_data: dict) -> dict:
    payload = req_data.copy()
    payload["kv_transfer_params"] = {
        "do_remote_decode": True,
        "do_remote_prefill": False,
        "remote_engine_id": None,
        "remote_block_ids": None,
        "remote_host": None,
        "remote_port": None,
    }
    payload["stream"] = False
    payload["max_tokens"] = 1
    payload["min_tokens"] = 1
    if "max_completion_tokens" in payload:
        payload["max_completion_tokens"] = 1
    payload.pop("stream_options", None)
    return payload

async def assign_instances(
    api: str,
    req_data: Any,
    request_length: int,
    *,
    is_initial_request: bool,
) -> InstanceInfo:
    runtime = get_runtime()
    args = get_global_args()
    prefiller_score = calculate_prefill_score(request_length)
    decoder_score = calculate_decode_score(request_length)
    request_id = next_req_id()
    pick_prefill = "begin_request" if is_initial_request else "reserve_prefill_kv"
    prefiller = await runtime.schedule(pick_prefill, prefiller_score)
    prefiller_key = prefiller["key"]

    try:
        response = await send_request_to_service(
            await runtime.get_client(ServerRole.PREFILL, prefiller_key),
            api,
            req_data,
            request_id,
            max_retries=args.max_retries,
            base_delay=args.retry_delay,
        )
    except Exception:
        await _abort_prefill_selection(runtime, prefiller_key, prefiller_score, is_initial_request=is_initial_request)
        raise

    kv_transfer_params = response.json().get("kv_transfer_params", {})
    if kv_transfer_params:
        req_data["kv_transfer_params"] = kv_transfer_params

    try:
        decoder = await runtime.schedule("pick_decoder", decoder_score)
    except Exception:
        await _abort_prefill_selection(runtime, prefiller_key, prefiller_score, is_initial_request=is_initial_request)
        raise

    prefiller_client = await runtime.get_client(ServerRole.PREFILL, prefiller_key)
    decoder_client = await runtime.get_client(ServerRole.DECODE, decoder["key"])
    logger.debug("Using %s %s", prefiller_client.base_url, decoder_client.base_url)
    return InstanceInfo(
        request_id=request_id,
        prefiller_key=prefiller_key,
        prefiller_score=prefiller_score,
        decoder_key=decoder["key"],
        decoder_score=decoder_score,
        decoder_host=decoder["host"],
        decoder_port=decoder["port"],
    )

def lookup(
        self,
        token_len: int,
        block_hashes: list[BlockHash],
        kv_cache_group_ids: list[int] | None = None,
    ) -> int:
        kv_cache_group_ids = kv_cache_group_ids or [0]
        hash_strs = [h.hex() for h in block_hashes]
        hash_frames = self.encoder.encode(hash_strs)
        kv_group_frames = self.encoder.encode(kv_cache_group_ids)
        token_len_bytes = token_len.to_bytes(4, byteorder="big")
        all_frames = [token_len_bytes] + list(kv_group_frames) + list(hash_frames)
        self.socket.send_multipart(all_frames, copy=False)
        resp = self.socket.recv()
        result = int.from_bytes(resp, "big")
        return result

data_flow: PROXY (one request arrives): load_balance_proxy_server_example.handle_completions_impl -> assign_instances picks a least-loaded prefiller via SharedProxyScheduler.begin_request (min-heap on active_tokens + 0.3*active_kv_cache).
PROXY->PREFILLER: build_prefill_request sets kv_transfer_params={do_remote_decode:True}, max_tokens=1; send_request_to_service POSTs to the prefiller. Prefiller runs prefill, computes full prompt KV.

PREFILLER scheduler: MooncakeLayerwiseConnectorScheduler.update_state_after_alloc sees do_remote_decode -> records the request in _reqs_need_send_layerwise. request_finished returns the handshake dict (remote_block_ids/engine_id/host/port) back to the proxy as kv_transfer_params.

PREFILLER worker (forward loop): for each transformer layer, save_kv_layer enqueues that layer's KV into KVCacheSendingLayerThread the moment it is computed (layer-by-layer push, overlapping with later layers' compute).

PROXY: assign_instances reads kv_transfer_params from the prefiller response, picks a least-loaded decoder via pick_decoder, forwards req (now carrying do_remote_prefill + remote handshake) to the decoder.

DECODER scheduler: get_num_new_matched_tokens sees do_remote_prefill -> returns count = full prompt len, load_async=True (pull whole prompt KV from the remote prefiller). update_state_after_alloc records it in _reqs_need_recv and POSTs to metaserver; build_connector_meta emits ReqMeta with the remote engine/host/port/block_ids.

DECODER worker: start_load_kv registers the recving request; a background recv thread pulls the KV over mooncake. KVCacheSendingLayerThread.get_transfer_meta turns (base_addr + block_id*block_len) into flat src/dst/length lists, group_concurrent_contiguous batches contiguous runs, and the mooncake TransferEngine (GlobalTE, backend 'ascend', P2PHANDSHAKE) performs the cross-node P2P copy.
DECODER worker: get_finished drains the recv thread's done set -> scheduler frees/uses blocks; decoder then generates tokens, streamed back through the proxy to the client.

★ AFFINITY (orthogonal pool path, the climax): when the external KV-pool connector is active, KVPoolScheduler.get_num_new_matched_tokens calls LookupKeyClient.lookup(token_len, block_hashes, group_ids) over zmq to the pool's lookup RPC. The pool returns how many of THIS request's prompt tokens are already cached externally (num_external_hit_tokens). need_to_allocate = hit - num_computed_tokens; vLLM allocates exactly that gap and loads from where the KV already lives, minimizing cross-node transfer. (Pool store/tick machinery = ch11.)

design_decisions.decision: Override vLLM's 'MultiConnector' factory entry with AscendMultiConnector instead of adding a new name.
design_decisions.why: Users keep configuring kv_connector='MultiConnector' (the upstream name); the registry pop+re-register transparently swaps in the HMA-aware ascend subclass without changing user config.

design_decisions.decision: In AscendMultiConnector.update_state_after_alloc, a MooncakeLayerwiseConnector ALWAYS receives the real allocated blocks, even when it is not the 'chosen' loader.
design_decisions.why: Base MultiConnector only hands real blocks to the single chosen loader and empty blocks to the rest (each request is loaded by exactly one connector). But layerwise PUSH is a producer-side save, not a load: the layerwise connector needs the real block ids to know where to read/transfer KV from, regardless of which connector won the load election.

design_decisions.decision: AscendMultiConnector subclasses SupportsHMA and implements request_finished_all_groups (per-kv-cache-group block ids) instead of the flat request_finished.
design_decisions.why: Hybrid Memory Allocator splits KV into multiple cache groups (e.g. full-attention + mamba/SWA) with different block sizes; freeing must be reported per group. The HMA path aggregates async-save counts across sub-connectors and asserts only one produces transfer params.

design_decisions.decision: MooncakeLayerwiseConnector is a thin facade that owns EITHER a Scheduler OR a Worker object selected by KVConnectorRole.
design_decisions.why: vLLM runs the connector in two processes: the scheduler process (decides what to load/save, builds metadata) and each worker process (touches the actual KV tensors). The same class is instantiated in both; role gates which half is live. This is the canonical vLLM v1 connector contract.

design_decisions.decision: Direction of transfer is driven entirely by request.kv_transfer_params flags: do_remote_prefill (this is the decoder, pull) vs do_remote_decode (this is the prefiller, push).
design_decisions.why: The same connector code runs on both P and D nodes; the proxy injects the flags so each node knows its role for a given request. The prefiller's request_finished returns a handshake dict that the proxy threads into the decoder's request.

design_decisions.decision: Layer-by-layer push: save_kv_layer enqueues each layer's KV the instant it is computed, rather than waiting for the whole prompt to finish.
design_decisions.why: Overlaps KV transfer of early layers with compute of later layers, hiding cross-node transfer latency. wait_for_save is a no-op precisely because saving is fire-and-forget per layer through the background send thread.

design_decisions.decision: One process-wide mooncake TransferEngine via double-checked-locked singleton GlobalTE, backend 'ascend' + 'P2PHANDSHAKE'.
design_decisions.why: The engine owns RDMA-style registered memory and RPC ports; sharing one per process avoids re-registering KV buffers and duplicate handshake ports across the send/recv threads.

design_decisions.decision: Proxy uses a lazy-deletion min-heap per role; prefill priority = active_tokens + 0.3*active_kv_cache, decode priority = active_tokens.
design_decisions.why: Prefill is compute-bound AND holds KV until the decoder pulls it, so KV pressure (active_kv_cache) is weighted in; decode is dominated by running token load. Lazy deletion (bump heap_seq, skip stale entries on pop) avoids O(n) heap rebuilds on every load change.

design_decisions.decision: ★ Cache-hit-aware (affinity) routing: ask the external KV pool 'how much of this prompt do you already have?' (lookup) BEFORE allocating, and load only the gap.
design_decisions.why: If the KV for a prefix already lives in the pool (possibly on the same node), recomputing or re-transferring it is wasted work/bandwidth. lookup returns the hit prefix length; need_to_allocate = hit - already_computed, so vLLM pulls exactly the missing tokens from where they live, minimizing cross-node transfer — the core PD-disaggregation saving.
design_decisions.evidence: pool_scheduler.py:L252-L293 (lookup -> need_to_allocate -> LoadSpec); L643-L658 (zmq lookup RPC)

design_decisions.decision: num_external_hit_tokens is decremented by 1 when it equals request.num_tokens.
design_decisions.why: vLLM requires at least one token to actually run through the model forward (you cannot schedule a request with zero new tokens to compute); so a full-prompt hit is clamped to leave one token to be (re)computed locally.

theory: PD (prefill/decode) disaggregation rationale: prefill is compute-bound (one big parallel pass over the whole prompt), decode is memory-bandwidth/latency-bound (many sequential single-token steps). Co-locating them makes each interfere with the other's batching. Splitting onto separate instances lets each scale and batch independently; the cost is moving the prompt's KV cache from the prefiller to the decoder once.

Transfer cost model: KV bytes per request ~= 2 (K,V) * num_layers * num_kv_heads * head_dim * dtype_size * num_prompt_tokens. For long prompts this dominates inter-node traffic, so two optimizations matter: (a) layer-by-layer pipelining overlaps transfer of layer i with compute of layer i+1 (hides ~(L-1)/L of transfer latency in the ideal case); (b) cache-hit-aware routing transfers only the (1 - hit_ratio) fraction not already cached at the destination/pool.

Affinity arithmetic (the climax, concrete): suppose a prompt has P=4000 prompt tokens, granularity g (cache_transfer_granularity) such that token_len = floor(4000/g)*g. lookup returns num_external_hit_tokens H (a multiple of the pool's block granularity). With num_computed_tokens=C already in vLLM's local cache, need_to_allocate = max(0, H - C). The request loads H-C tokens from the pool and computes the remaining P-H locally; cross-node bytes scale with (H-C), not P. If H == request.num_tokens, H is clamped to H-1 so >=1 token runs the forward.

Least-loaded heap routing: with K servers, _pick_server is O(log K) amortized (heap push/pop) plus lazy-deletion skips; the heap is rebuilt only when it grows past 2*K stale entries, bounding memory and keeping average pick cheap under churn.

Block coalescing: group_concurrent_contiguous turns N individual block copies into M<=N batched transfers by detecting runs where BOTH local and remote block ids increase by 1; mooncake then issues one P2P op per run, cutting per-transfer overhead.

delete.what: All NPU-specific KV resharding / quantization paths in save_kv_layer and get_transfer_meta (pd_head_ratio!=1, enable_kv_quant, enable_c8_quant, MambaSpec/conv-ssm address math, npu_paged_cache_load, kv_alltoall_and_rearrange, trans_nd_to_nz, npu_stream_switch).
delete.why_safe: These are hardware/feature variants of the SAME control flow (compute keys/values for a layer -> enqueue a SendTask). The book teaches the connector contract + layer-by-layer push + address-math principle; the simplified version keeps the default (pd_head_ratio==1, no quant) branch which is the readable core. Host has no NPU so these cannot run anyway.

delete.what: TLS/ssl handshake plumbing in MooncakeLayerwiseConnectorScheduler.__init__ (tls_config, httpx cert/verify) and the metaserver retry/backoff internals of _access_metaserver.
delete.why_safe: Transport hardening, orthogonal to the P/D routing logic. The POST-handshake-to-metaserver step is kept conceptually (one call); its retry/SSL details don't change the data flow.

delete.what: Hybrid-KV-cache-group machinery in KVPoolScheduler that ch10 does not use: _uses_hybrid_kv_cache, _infer_mamba_groups, _infer_swa_blocks, get_sw_clipped_blocks, touch_sending_mamba_blocks, update_connector_output, build_connector_meta body, request_finished*, the whole store/save side.
delete.why_safe: ch10 references ONLY the cache-hit query (get_num_new_matched_tokens + LookupKeyClient.lookup) for affinity routing. The store/pool tick machinery is explicitly deferred to ch11; including it here would both over-delete-risk and pre-empt ch11. Keep only the lookup->need_to_allocate->LoadSpec path.

delete.what: Proxy operational endpoints unrelated to dispatch: NodeListener / instance add-remove-drain (adjust_instances_impl, activate/drop waiting, taint/finalize), SchedulerManager/BaseManager multiprocessing wiring, WorkerRuntime client pooling, lifespan, auth_headers, manager_config file IO.
delete.why_safe: These are deployment/elasticity scaffolding. The chapter's point is the request DISPATCH decision (score -> least-loaded pick -> prefill -> handshake -> decoder). Keep SharedProxyScheduler core (pools, _priority, _pick_server, begin_request/pick_decoder/release) + assign_instances skeleton.

delete.what: KVCacheSendingLayerThread/_RecvingLayerThread full bodies beyond get_transfer_meta's default branch; SizedDict, MooncakeAgentMetadata, zmq retry helpers, _get_remote_socket pooling.
delete.why_safe: Threading/socket lifecycle is real but not the teaching target. Keep get_transfer_meta's base-addr+block_len arithmetic and group_concurrent_contiguous (the 'how addresses are computed' core); reduce the thread to 'pop SendTask -> compute meta -> engine.transfer' shape.

must_keep.why: Shows the factory override that makes 'MultiConnector' resolve to AscendMultiConnector and registers the three mooncake connectors by name — the dispatch entry point.
The subclass at the heart of layer 1; reader must see what it adds over base MultiConnector.
The one method where Ascend diverges from base (layerwise always gets real blocks); load-bearing contrast. Present in both AscendMultiConnector and the layerwise scheduler with different roles.
The HMA hook that justifies subclassing SupportsHMA; must be visible.
Base class; the 'load from first matching, save to all' fan-out is the thing Ascend extends.
Appears in BOTH the connector election (MultiConnector/layerwise: do_remote_prefill pull) AND the affinity climax (KVPoolScheduler: lookup-based hit). Central to the chapter.
The chosen connector taught in depth; the facade.
Scheduler-side half: recv/send routing + handshake.
Worker-side half: the callbacks embedded in the forward loop.
The scheduler->worker metadata channel; the 'metadata' in 'connector role + metadata + callbacks'.
Per-request payload carrying local + remote block ids/host/port — the concrete handshake content.
Where pending recv/send become per-step metadata; the scheduler->worker bridge.
Worker load callback that the forward loop calls before execution (consumer pull registration / producer mapping resolution).
Worker save callback called per layer = the layer-by-layer push, the defining feature of THIS connector.
Closes the async loop: reports completed transfers so blocks can be freed.
The flag that means 'I am the decoder, pull the prompt KV'; reader must track it.
The flag that means 'I am the prefiller, push KV to a decoder'; the mirror of the above.
The mooncake TransferEngine singleton — layer 2's entry object.
Shows the P2PHANDSHAKE/'ascend' init that defines the P2P transport.
Turns block ids into absolute src/dst addresses = the concrete 'how KV moves between nodes'.
The coalescing optimization that batches contiguous block transfers.
Layer 3's load balancer object.
Concrete prefill scoring formula used in the dispatch trace.
Concrete decode scoring formula.
The least-loaded min-heap pick — the dispatch decision.

Where the proxy STAMPS the P role onto a request: sets kv_transfer_params={do_remote_decode:True, do_remote_prefill:False}, max_tokens=1, min_tokens=1. This is the concrete 'you are the prefiller' instruction — the data_flow step-2 payload reader/writer must see.
The caller that applies build_prefill_request and POSTs the prefill-only request to the chosen prefiller (named in data_flow step 2). Keeps the build->POST link explicit.
The end-to-end prefill->handshake->decoder orchestration the chapter narrates.
★ Owner of the affinity climax.
★ The lookup RPC client; the cache-hit query mechanism.
★ The exact call (token_len, block_hashes, group_ids) -> hit count that drives affinity routing.
★ Records vllm_cached vs kvpool_cached tokens — the numbers the climax's affinity arithmetic uses.
★ The computed gap (hit - computed) = exactly how many tokens get pulled; the punchline number.

diagram_plan.name: pd-three-layers-overview
diagram_plan.purpose: Roadmap 'you are here' + the 3-layer stack: proxy/router (top) -> connector dispatch (AscendMultiConnector -> 3 mooncake connectors) -> mooncake P2P transfer (bottom), with the affinity-lookup callout on the connector layer.
diagram_plan.svg_spec: Three horizontal bands (Proxy / Connector dispatch / P2P transfer). In the connector band show AscendMultiConnector fanning to MooncakeConnector, MooncakeHybridConnector, MooncakeLayerwiseConnector (highlight layerwise). Side callout: KVPoolScheduler.lookup -> affinity. Roadmap highlight node: ch10.

diagram_plan.name: pd-request-lifecycle-sequence
diagram_plan.purpose: One request's life across Proxy / Prefiller / Decoder as a sequence diagram — the data_flow narrated visually.
diagram_plan.svg_spec: Lifelines: Client, Proxy(SharedProxyScheduler), Prefiller(scheduler+worker), Decoder(scheduler+worker). Arrows: pick prefiller -> prefill(max_tokens=1, do_remote_decode) -> save_kv_layer per layer (loop) -> handshake kv_transfer_params back to proxy -> pick decoder -> forward(do_remote_prefill) -> start_load_kv + mooncake pull -> get_finished -> stream tokens. Annotate layer-by-layer push as an overlapping loop.

diagram_plan.name: connector-scheduler-worker-split
diagram_plan.purpose: Show the role split: same MooncakeLayerwiseConnector class, Scheduler half (scheduler proc) vs Worker half (worker proc), and which base-class hooks land where.
diagram_plan.svg_spec: Two boxes (SCHEDULER role / WORKER role) under one facade. Scheduler box: get_num_new_matched_tokens, update_state_after_alloc, build_connector_meta, request_finished. Worker box: start_load_kv, save_kv_layer, get_finished. Dashed arrow = MooncakeLayerwiseConnectorMetadata flowing scheduler->worker.

diagram_plan.name: affinity-hit-routing-numbers
diagram_plan.purpose: ★ The climax: a concrete token-count walk from lookup hit to need_to_allocate to bytes-saved.
diagram_plan.svg_spec: A horizontal token bar of length P (prompt). Segment [0,C]=locally computed, [C,H]=external-hit-loaded-from-pool (need_to_allocate=H-C), [H,P]=computed locally. Side panel: lookup(token_len, block_hashes) -> H; need_to_allocate=max(0,H-C); clamp H-=1 if H==num_tokens. Caption: cross-node bytes ~ (H-C), not P.

foreshadow_due.note: bible.py due ch10 returned empty (no registered foreshadow/payoff items for ch10). Forward-reference to plant in passing: KVPoolScheduler's store/pool-tick machinery (sending_events, build_connector_meta save side, pool worker) is deferred to ch11 — ch10 only borrows its cache-hit lookup for affinity routing.
